description: CLI interface for the Sectigo affiliate program

info.help: Show verified metadata for this CLI.
info.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo program info` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

program.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo program` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth login` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth logout` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test`` (API/OAuth) or via the live browser check (browser_session).

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth status` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth test` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth profiles list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth profiles get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth profiles create` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth profiles set-default` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth profiles delete` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth profiles` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo auth` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo cache clear` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `sectigo cache` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

usage_instructions: Use `` with the documented arguments and options shown below.
